elements.name: hx-partial
elements.description: Routes response content to a specific target. Use `hx-target` or `id` to choose the destination and `hx-swap` to choose the swap style.

attributes.name: hx-get
attributes.description: Issues a `GET` request to the specified URL and swaps the response into the DOM.

attributes.name: hx-post
attributes.description: Issues a `POST` request to the specified URL and swaps the response into the DOM.

attributes.name: hx-put
attributes.description: Issues a `PUT` request to the specified URL and swaps the response into the DOM.

attributes.name: hx-patch
attributes.description: Issues a `PATCH` request to the specified URL and swaps the response into the DOM.

attributes.name: hx-delete
attributes.description: Issues a `DELETE` request to the specified URL and swaps the response into the DOM.

attributes.name: hx-trigger
attributes.description: Controls which event specifications trigger an element's request. Supports filters and modifiers such as `delay`, `throttle`, `once`, `changed`, `from`, and `target`.

attributes.name: hx-swap
attributes.description: Controls how response content is inserted. Supports swap styles such as `innerHTML`, `outerHTML`, `textContent`, `beforebegin`, `afterbegin`, `beforeend`, `afterend`, `innerMorph`, `outerMorph`, `outerSync`, `delete`, `none`, and extension-provided styles.

description-sections.Inheritance: Use the :inherited modifier (for example, `hx-swap:inherited`) to apply this attribute to htmx requests in child elements.

attributes.name: hx-target
attributes.description: Specifies where to insert the response content. Accepts CSS selectors, `this`, and htmx extended selectors such as `closest`, `find`, `next`, and `previous`.

description-sections.Inheritance: Use the :inherited modifier (for example, `hx-target:inherited`) to apply this attribute to htmx requests in child elements.

attributes.name: hx-select
attributes.description: Selects the part of the response to swap into the target. The value is a CSS selector.

description-sections.Inheritance: Use the :inherited modifier (for example, `hx-select:inherited`) to apply this attribute to htmx requests in child elements.

attributes.name: hx-on
attributes.description: Wires inline JavaScript to run when an event fires. Use `hx-on:event` for any DOM event, or `hx-on::event` as shorthand for `hx-on:htmx:event`.

attributes.name: hx-vals
attributes.description: Adds values to request parameters. Accepts HCON/JSON, and can evaluate JavaScript expressions with the `js:` prefix.

description-sections.Inheritance: Use the :inherited modifier (for example, `hx-vals:inherited`) to apply this attribute to htmx requests in child elements.

attributes.name: hx-include
attributes.description: Includes additional element values in the request. Accepts CSS selectors, `this`, and htmx extended selectors.

description-sections.Inheritance: Use the :inherited modifier (for example, `hx-include:inherited`) to apply this attribute to htmx requests in child elements.

attributes.name: hx-swap-oob
attributes.description: Marks response elements to swap into the page out of band, outside the normal target.

attributes.name: hx-select-oob
attributes.description: Selects response elements to swap into the page out of band.

description-sections.Inheritance: Use the :inherited modifier (for example, `hx-select-oob:inherited`) to apply this attribute to htmx requests in child elements.

attributes.name: hx-push-url
attributes.description: Pushes a URL into browser history after a request. Accepts `true`, `false`, or a URL.

description-sections.Inheritance: Use the :inherited modifier (for example, `hx-push-url:inherited`) to apply this attribute to htmx requests in child elements.

attributes.name: hx-replace-url
attributes.description: Replaces the current browser history URL after a request. Accepts `true`, `false`, or a URL.

description-sections.Inheritance: Use the :inherited modifier (for example, `hx-replace-url:inherited`) to apply this attribute to htmx requests in child elements.

attributes.name: hx-headers
attributes.description: Adds custom headers to the request. Accepts HCON/JSON, and can evaluate JavaScript expressions with the `js:` prefix.

description-sections.Inheritance: Use the :inherited modifier (for example, `hx-headers:inherited`) to apply this attribute to htmx requests in child elements.

attributes.name: hx-encoding
attributes.description: Sets the request encoding type. Use `multipart/form-data` for file uploads.

description-sections.Inheritance: Use the :inherited modifier (for example, `hx-encoding:inherited`) to apply this attribute to htmx requests in child elements.

attributes.name: hx-indicator
attributes.description: Specifies the element or elements that receive the `htmx-request` class while the request is in flight.

description-sections.Inheritance: Use the :inherited modifier (for example, `hx-indicator:inherited`) to apply this attribute to htmx requests in child elements.

attributes.name: hx-boost
attributes.description: Converts eligible links and forms to htmx-powered requests while preserving progressive enhancement. Can carry boost-specific request config.

description-sections.Inheritance: Use the :inherited modifier (for example, `hx-boost:inherited`) to apply this attribute to htmx requests in child elements.

attributes.name: hx-sync
attributes.description: Synchronizes requests between elements using strategies such as `drop`, `abort`, `replace`, `queue first`, `queue last`, and `queue all`.

description-sections.Inheritance: Use the :inherited modifier (for example, `hx-sync:inherited`) to apply this attribute to htmx requests in child elements.

attributes.name: hx-confirm
attributes.description: Shows a confirmation dialog before issuing a request. A `js:` value can run custom confirmation logic.

description-sections.Inheritance: Use the :inherited modifier (for example, `hx-confirm:inherited`) to apply this attribute to htmx requests in child elements.

attributes.name: hx-validate
attributes.description: Runs HTML5 constraint validation before sending the request.

attributes.name: hx-disable
attributes.description: Disables selected elements while the request is in flight.

description-sections.Inheritance: Use the :inherited modifier (for example, `hx-disable:inherited`) to apply this attribute to htmx requests in child elements.

attributes.name: hx-ignore
attributes.description: Disables htmx processing for the element and its descendants.

attributes.name: hx-preserve
attributes.description: Preserves an element with a stable `id` across swaps when the response contains a matching element.

attributes.name: hx-preload
attributes.description: Preloads content before the user triggers a request. Requires the preload extension.

description-sections.Inheritance: Use the :inherited modifier (for example, `hx-preload:inherited`) to apply this attribute to htmx requests in child elements.

attributes.name: hx-optimistic
attributes.description: Shows optimistic UI content from a template while a request is in flight. Requires the optimistic extension.

attributes.name: hx-status
attributes.description: Overrides swap behavior for specific HTTP response status codes. Supports exact codes such as `404` and wildcard patterns such as `50x` and `5xx`.

attributes.name: hx-action
attributes.description: Specifies the URL that receives the request. Use with `hx-method` or as the shared action for verb-specific attributes.

attributes.name: hx-method
attributes.description: Specifies the HTTP method to use with `hx-action`. Valid methods are `GET`, `POST`, `PUT`, `PATCH`, and `DELETE`.

attributes.name: hx-config
attributes.description: Configures per-request behavior using HCON or JSON. Values are merged into the request config; `mode` is always reset to the global `htmx.config.mode`.

description-sections.Inheritance: Use the :inherited modifier (for example, `hx-config:inherited`) to apply this attribute to htmx requests in child elements.

attributes.name: hx-history-elt
attributes.description: Marks the element that htmx swaps when restoring browser history. If absent, htmx falls back to the body element.

attributes.name: hx-sse:connect
attributes.description: Establishes a persistent Server-Sent Events connection. Defaults to loading immediately, reconnecting with exponential backoff, and pausing on background tabs.

attributes.name: hx-sse:close
attributes.description: Closes an SSE connection when a named event matching the attribute value is received from the server.

attributes.name: hx-ws:connect
attributes.description: Establishes a WebSocket connection to the specified URL. Requires the WebSocket extension.

attributes.name: hx-ws:send
attributes.description: Sends form data and `hx-vals` to a WebSocket when the element is triggered. Requires the WebSocket extension.

attributes.name: hx-head
attributes.description: Controls how the head-support extension merges or appends response `<head>` content.

attributes.name: hx-browser-indicator
attributes.description: Enables the browser native loading indicator for this element's requests when set to `true`. Requires the browser-indicator extension.

attributes.name: hx-targets
attributes.description: Swaps the same response content into all elements matching the selector. Requires the targets extension.

description-sections.Inheritance: Use the :inherited modifier (for example, `hx-targets:inherited`) to apply this attribute to htmx requests in child elements.

attributes.name: hx-ptag
attributes.description: Sets the initial per-element polling tag sent as the `HX-PTag` request header. Requires the ptag extension.

attributes.name: hx-history
attributes.description: Opts an element or page out of the history-cache extension when set to `false`.

attributes.name: hx-nonce
attributes.description: Marks htmx elements as server-rendered trusted markup for the CSP extension nonce gate.

attributes.name: hx-live
attributes.description: Runs a reactive expression whenever hx-live observes relevant DOM or state changes. Requires the hx-live extension.

attributes.name: hx-live:*
attributes.description: Binds a reactive expression to an attribute, property, class, style, text, or HTML update. Requires the hx-live extension.

attributes.name: hx-prompt
attributes.description: Prompts before a request, sends the answer as the `HX-Prompt` header, and aborts when the user cancels. Requires the prompt extension.

description-sections.Inheritance: Use the :inherited modifier (for example, `hx-prompt:inherited`) to apply this attribute to htmx requests in child elements.

classes.name: htmx-added
classes.description: Applied to newly inserted content before settling, then removed after the settle phase.

classes.name: htmx-indicator
classes.description: Default indicator class hidden by htmx CSS until a containing or matching element receives `htmx-request`.

classes.name: htmx-request
classes.description: Applied to the request indicator elements while a request is in flight.

classes.name: htmx-settling
classes.description: Applied to a target during the settle phase, then removed after settling completes.

classes.name: htmx-swapping
classes.description: Applied to a target during the swap delay, then removed when content is inserted.

classes.name: hx-optimistic
classes.description: Applied to optimistic UI content inserted by the hx-optimistic extension.

events.name: intersect
events.description: Event emitted when an element observed by `hx-trigger="intersect"` enters the viewport.

htmx-events.name: abort
htmx-events.description: Listened for by htmx. Trigger `htmx:abort` on an element with an in-flight request to abort that request.

htmx-events.name: before:init
htmx-events.description: Fires before htmx initializes a specific element. Event detail is empty.

htmx-events.name: after:init
htmx-events.description: Fires after htmx initializes a specific element. Event detail is empty.

htmx-events.name: before:process
htmx-events.description: Fires before htmx processes a DOM node. Event detail is empty.

htmx-events.name: after:process
htmx-events.description: Fires after htmx processes a DOM node. Event detail is empty.

htmx-events.name: after:implicitInheritance
htmx-events.description: Fires after implicit inheritance is applied to an element.

htmx-events.name: before:cleanup
htmx-events.description: Fires before htmx removes listeners and internal data from an element. Event detail is empty.

htmx-events.name: after:cleanup
htmx-events.description: Fires after htmx removes listeners and internal data from an element. Event detail is empty.

htmx-events.name: config:request
htmx-events.description: Fires after request parameters are collected and before the request is issued. `detail.ctx` is the request context and can be modified.

htmx-events.name: confirm
htmx-events.description: Fires when confirmation is required. `detail.ctx` is the request context; call `preventDefault()` and `detail.issueRequest()` for custom dialogs.

htmx-events.name: before:request
htmx-events.description: Fires immediately before `fetch()` is called. `detail.ctx` is the request context. Cancel to stop the request.

htmx-events.name: before:response
htmx-events.description: Fires after response headers are received but before the body is consumed. `detail.ctx.response.raw` is the unconsumed `Response`.

htmx-events.name: after:request
htmx-events.description: Fires after the response body is consumed. `detail.ctx` contains request, response, and text data.

htmx-events.name: response:error
htmx-events.description: Fires when an HTTP error status code (400 or higher) is received. `detail.ctx.response.status` contains the status code.

htmx-events.name: finally:request
htmx-events.description: Always fires at the end of the request lifecycle. `detail.ctx` is the request context.

htmx-events.name: error
htmx-events.description: Fires when a network, request, swap, or JavaScript error occurs. `detail.error` contains the error when available.

htmx-events.name: before:swap
htmx-events.description: Fires before content is swapped. `detail.ctx` is the request context and `detail.tasks` contains the swap tasks. Cancel to skip the swap.

htmx-events.name: after:swap
htmx-events.description: Fires after content is swapped into the DOM. `detail.ctx` is the request context.

htmx-events.name: finally:swap
htmx-events.description: Fires after a swap attempt finishes, even when the swap throws or is skipped. `detail.ctx` is the request context.

htmx-events.name: before:settle
htmx-events.description: Fires before the settle phase. `detail.task`, `detail.newContent`, and `detail.settleTasks` describe the settle work.

htmx-events.name: after:settle
htmx-events.description: Fires after the settle phase completes. `detail.task`, `detail.newContent`, and `detail.settleTasks` describe the settled work.

htmx-events.name: before:history:update
htmx-events.description: Fires before htmx pushes or replaces browser history. The detail contains the history update information.

htmx-events.name: after:history:update
htmx-events.description: Fires after htmx pushes or replaces browser history. The detail contains the history update information.

htmx-events.name: after:history:push
htmx-events.description: Fires after htmx pushes a new browser history entry. `detail.path` is the pushed path.

htmx-events.name: after:history:replace
htmx-events.description: Fires after htmx replaces the current browser history entry. `detail.path` is the replaced path.

htmx-events.name: before:history:restore
htmx-events.description: Fires before htmx restores a history entry. `detail.path` is the restored path and `detail.cacheMiss` is set for core re-fetch restores.

htmx-events.name: before:viewTransition
htmx-events.description: Fires before a View Transition API swap task starts. `detail.task` is the swap task.

htmx-events.name: after:viewTransition
htmx-events.description: Fires after a View Transition API swap task completes. `detail.task` is the swap task.

htmx-events.name: before:sse:connection
htmx-events.description: Fires before an SSE connection attempt. `detail.connection` can be modified; set `cancelled` or cancel the event to stop connecting.

htmx-events.name: after:sse:connection
htmx-events.description: Fires after a successful SSE connection or reconnection. `detail.connection` describes the connection.

htmx-events.name: sse:close
htmx-events.description: Fires when an SSE connection closes. `detail.connection` and `detail.reason` describe the close.

htmx-events.name: sse:error
htmx-events.description: Fires when an SSE stream error occurs. `detail.error`, `detail.url`, and sometimes `detail.status` describe the failure.

htmx-events.name: before:sse:message
htmx-events.description: Fires before an SSE message is processed. `detail.message` contains `data`, `event`, `id`, and `cancelled`.

htmx-events.name: after:sse:message
htmx-events.description: Fires after an SSE message is processed. `detail.message` contains `data`, `event`, and `id`.

htmx-events.name: before:ws:connection
htmx-events.description: Fires before a WebSocket connection attempt. `detail.connection` can be modified; set `cancelled` or cancel the event to stop connecting.

htmx-events.name: after:ws:connection
htmx-events.description: Fires after a successful WebSocket connection. `detail.connection` describes the connection.

htmx-events.name: ws:close
htmx-events.description: Fires when a WebSocket connection closes. `detail.connection`, `detail.reason`, and `detail.code` describe the close.

htmx-events.name: ws:error
htmx-events.description: Fires on WebSocket connection or send errors. `detail.url` and `detail.error` describe the failure.

htmx-events.name: before:ws:request
htmx-events.description: Fires before sending a WebSocket message. `detail.headers` and `detail.body` are modifiable. Cancel to skip sending.

htmx-events.name: after:ws:request
htmx-events.description: Fires after a WebSocket message is sent. `detail.headers` and `detail.body` contain the sent payload.

htmx-events.name: before:ws:message
htmx-events.description: Fires before a WebSocket message is processed. `detail.message.text`, `detail.message.json`, and `detail.message.cancelled` are available.

htmx-events.name: after:ws:message
htmx-events.description: Fires after a WebSocket message is processed. `detail.message.text` and `detail.message.json` are available.

htmx-events.name: download:start
htmx-events.description: Fires when download response headers are received. `detail.total` is the content length or `null`.

htmx-events.name: download:progress
htmx-events.description: Fires for each download chunk. `detail.loaded`, `detail.total`, and `detail.percent` describe progress.

htmx-events.name: download:complete
htmx-events.description: Fires after a file download is triggered in the browser. `detail.filename` and `detail.size` describe the downloaded blob.

htmx-events.name: before:head:merge
htmx-events.description: Fires before the head-support extension processes response `<head>` content. `detail.ctx` is the request context.

htmx-events.name: before:head:add
htmx-events.description: Fires before the head-support extension adds a new head element. `detail.headElement` is the element. Cancel to skip it.

htmx-events.name: before:head:remove
htmx-events.description: Fires before the head-support extension removes a head element. `detail.headElement` is the element. Cancel to keep it.

htmx-events.name: after:head:merge
htmx-events.description: Fires after the head-support extension merges head content. `detail.added`, `detail.kept`, and `detail.removed` list affected elements.

htmx-events.name: history:cache:before:save
htmx-events.description: Fires before the history-cache extension saves the current page. Cancel or set `detail.cancelled` to skip saving.

htmx-events.name: history:cache:after:save
htmx-events.description: Fires after the history-cache extension saves the current page.

htmx-events.name: history:cache:miss
htmx-events.description: Fires when the requested history entry is not in the cache. Set `detail.refreshOnMiss = true` to force a full reload.

htmx-events.name: history:cache:hit
htmx-events.description: Fires when a cache entry is found. Cancel to bypass the cache and let core fetch from the server.

htmx-events.name: history:cache:before:restore
htmx-events.description: Fires before cached content is restored. `detail.head` may be changed and `detail.ready` may delay the restore.

htmx-events.name: history:cache:after:restore
htmx-events.description: Fires after cached content is restored. `detail.item` is the restored cache item.

htmx-events.name: security:strip
htmx-events.description: Fires when the CSP extension strips htmx attributes from an element. `detail.reason` and `detail.stripped` describe the action.

htmx-events.name: security:violation
htmx-events.description: Fires when the CSP extension blocks an unsafe htmx action. `detail.reason` describes the violation.

htmx-events.name: prompt
htmx-events.description: Fires after the prompt extension receives an answer. `detail.prompt` is the answer and `detail.target` is the target. Cancel to abort.
